note: Subset of App Store Review Guidelines that apply to Safari Web Extensions packaged inside a containing app, plus Safari-specific 4.4 extension rules.

policies.title: 1.6 Data Security - implement appropriate security measures
policies.fix: Implement secure handling of user information and prevent unauthorized access by third parties.

policies.title: 2.1 App Completeness - submit final, fully-functional builds
policies.fix: Remove placeholder content. Ensure URLs, in-app purchases, and demo accounts work for the reviewer.

policies.title: 2.3.1 Accurate Metadata - no hidden or undocumented features
policies.fix: Document every feature in the App Review notes; do not ship hidden or dormant functionality.

policies.title: 2.3.7 Unique app name and accurate keywords
policies.fix: Choose a unique app name (<=30 chars). Do not stuff trademarks or popular app names into metadata.

policies.title: 2.3.10 Focus on Apple platforms - no other-platform branding
policies.fix: Remove names, icons, or imagery of other mobile platforms or alternative app marketplaces from metadata.

policies.title: 2.5.1 Use only public APIs on the current shipping OS
policies.fix: Use public Apple APIs only. Phase out deprecated frameworks.

policies.title: 2.5.2 Self-contained - no remote code execution
policies.fix: Bundle every script in the package. Apple disallows downloading executable code at runtime.

policies.title: 2.5.3 No malware, viruses, or disruptive code
policies.fix: Confirm no malware or code that disrupts the OS, hardware, push notifications, or Game Center.

policies.title: 2.5.6 Web browsing must use WebKit
policies.fix: Apps that browse the web must use WebKit and WebKit JavaScript (entitlements required for alternatives).

policies.title: 2.5.14 Recording requires explicit user consent and indicator
policies.fix: Request explicit consent and provide a visible/audible indication when recording user activity.

policies.title: 4.2 Minimum Functionality - more than a webpage wrapper
policies.fix: Provide meaningful, lasting utility beyond a repackaged website.

policies.title: 4.3 Spam - no near-duplicates, saturated categories
policies.fix: Do not submit duplicate or near-duplicate apps; differentiate via in-app purchase variations instead.

policies.title: 4.4 Extensions - host app must comply with extension docs
policies.fix: Comply with the Safari Web Extensions documentation; disclose extensions in marketing text.

policies.title: 4.4.2 Safari extensions - run on current Safari, no malicious content, narrow website access
policies.fix: Limit host permissions to specific origins. Safari extensions must not claim more website access than necessary.

policies.title: 4.5.4 Push Notifications - opt-in, not required for function
policies.fix: Push notifications must be opt-in; do not use them for promotions without explicit consent and an opt-out.

policies.title: 5.1.1(i) Privacy Policy - link in App Store Connect and in-app
policies.fix: Provide a privacy policy URL in the manifest and the App Store listing; describe data collected and retention.

policies.title: 5.1.1(ii) Permission - secure user consent for any data collection
policies.fix: Secure user consent for any user/usage data collection, even if anonymized.

policies.title: 5.1.1(iii) Data Minimization - collect only what is needed
policies.fix: Request only data relevant to the core functionality. Use share sheets/pickers instead of broad access where possible.

policies.title: 5.1.1(v) Account Sign-In - allow use without an account where possible; offer in-app account deletion
policies.fix: Do not require login for non-account features; if accounts are supported, provide in-app deletion.

policies.title: 5.1.2 Data Use and Sharing - explicit consent for sharing/tracking
policies.fix: Obtain explicit ATT permission for tracking and disclose all third-party sharing.

policies.title: 5.1.4 Kids - no third-party analytics/ads in kids apps
policies.fix: If targeted at kids, comply with COPPA/GDPR-K and remove third-party analytics/ads (limited exceptions).

policies.title: 5.1.5 Location Services - only when directly relevant
policies.fix: Request geolocation only when needed for a disclosed feature; explain purpose in your purpose string.

policies.title: 5.2.1 Intellectual Property - originals or licensed content only
policies.fix: Use only content you created or are licensed to use; legal entity submitter must own/license rights.

policies.title: 5.2.3 Audio/Video Downloading - no unauthorized downloads
policies.fix: Do not save/convert/download media from third-party services without explicit authorization.

policies.title: 5.6 Developer Code of Conduct - no manipulation, fraud, or harassment
policies.fix: Treat customers and Apple respectfully. Repeated manipulative behavior leads to program removal.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Review 2.5.2): downloading/executing code at runtime
policies.fix: Apple rejects extensions that load executable code from a remote source. Bundle and statically declare every script.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Review 4.4.2): Safari extension requests access to all websites
policies.fix: Limit host_permissions to the specific origins where your extension provides functionality. Document why each origin is required.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Review 5.1.1): privacy policy missing or does not cover the extension
policies.fix: Provide a privacy policy URL in App Store Connect AND inside the app. The policy must explicitly describe data collected by the Safari extension.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Review 2.3.10): metadata mentions other browsers / platforms
policies.fix: Strip references to Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Android, etc. from screenshots, description, and in-app UI for the App Store build.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Review 4.2): containing app does little beyond enabling the extension
policies.fix: Ensure the macOS/iOS containing app has standalone value (settings, onboarding, account management) — not just an "Enable in Safari" button.

policies.title: Reported rejection (App Tracking Transparency): tracking without ATT prompt
policies.fix: If the extension or containing app tracks users across other companies’ apps/sites, present the ATT prompt and respect the response.
